prep.summary: Review the April 2nd alignment session with Thomas—you advised him to address political friction with Larry Abel and Dan Griffiths directly, partner closely with Mike Halleck, and prove the Policy Summary database's value. Analyze the May 28th POC sync with Larry Abel—the session focused on streamlining the ServiceNow monitoring narrative for Dan Griffiths and evaluating potential vendor lock-in.
tasks: Reconcile the ongoing professional friction between Thomas and Larry Abel.
Confirm if Thomas is now regularly included in Mike Halleck's solutioning meetings.
Audit the current technical strategy documentation for the Policy Summary database.
Determine the next steps for the ServiceNow POC following the May 28th presentation alignment.
prep.title: Thomas - Monthly 1on1

prep.summary: You must prepare for your sync with Praveena by reviewing the June 1st meeting—which focused on mid-year performance calibration, Matt's transition, and Amit's workload—and addressing the outstanding justification for Jon's backfill request. No work notes exist for your last meeting with John. For the Business Process Health Scorecard, you should contact Brian Simpkin and Sean Garner—the key owners driving the Scan AI integration and business case.
tasks: Confirm the finalization of Matt's transition plan and the status of Amit's backup plan.
Draft the backfill justification for Jon using the resource capacity gaps left by Matt's departure.
Contact Brian Simpkin and Sean Garner to secure help with the Business Process Health Scorecard.
Verify the details of your last interaction with John since no direct meeting notes were located.
prep.title: Bi-weekly with Praveena

prep.summary: You met with Suresh on June 12 to align on the Conversational Quote App—a proprietary initiative prioritizing internal API integration over third-party platforms. No individual meeting notes exist for Ramesh—though Suresh designated him to the core working group tasked with driving this conversational effort. You must now navigate resource constraints—specifically Chuck Monro's availability—while TCS conducts their free four-to-six-week feasibility study.
tasks: Monitor the TCS feasibility study to ensure successful integration with internal Pub Site or Expanded Distribution APIs.
Establish the weekly meeting cadence for the working group comprising Ramesh, Casey, Seema, Mark, and Nigam Naik.
Address vendor tool access constraints directly with Chuck Monro to avoid launch delays.
Align with the Member Value Organization to manage external stakeholder expectations for the quoting application.
prep.title: Monthly sync with Suresh

prep.summary: You must unify the fragmented process telemetry landscape—specifically consolidating ServiceNow, Celonis, and Scan AI—into a single Process Health Scorecard to eliminate operational blind spots. Your recent efforts focus heavily on technical modernization—driving the Apache Flink Q3 2026 rollout, building the Scan AI procurement RFP, and executing the Life Company strategic pivot. Address these integration overlaps directly to prevent costly vendor lock-in and establish clear architectural guardrails across P&C.
tasks: Finalize the Enterprise Architecture position paper to define clear operational boundaries between ServiceNow, Celonis, and Scan AI.
Gather cross-functional requirements from Bank and P&C to build the business case for the Scan AI express RFP.
Reformat the ServiceNow POC presentation deck for Dan Griffiths by hiding slides 6, 7, 15, and 515 to streamline the narrative.
Initiate the weekly stakeholder meeting cadence to maintain momentum on the Q3 process health pilot.
Align the P&C funding timeline with the Apache Flink deployment targets scheduled for late 2026.
prep.title: P&C IT EMG Planning Session
